findings.description: The PDF is a map figure with spatial layout showing sampling locations, legends, and data callout boxes positioned geographically. The HTML renders this as a linearized sequence of text blocks and tables, completely losing the map-based spatial layout.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a geographic map with sample points (SW/SD-1 through SW/SD-5) positioned spatially with callout boxes. HTML converts everything to a linear top-to-bottom flow with a small thumbnail image and then separate tables for each sample point.

findings.description: The main map image is reduced to a very small, low-resolution thumbnail in the HTML, losing all legibility of the geographic features, roads, property boundaries, and sample location markers.
findings.evidence: PDF has a full-page detailed map. HTML shows a tiny thumbnail at the top that is essentially unreadable.

findings.description: The data tables for each sampling location (SW-1 through SW-5) are present in the HTML and contain the correct data values, though they are rendered as separate standalone tables rather than callout boxes on the map.
findings.evidence: PDF shows data in callout boxes (e.g., SW-2: Tetrachloroethene 6.07, Copper 4.85). HTML shows these same values in structured tables.

findings.description: The reading order in HTML follows a reasonable sequence (title info, legend items, then sample data tables in order SW-1 through SW-5, then notes), which preserves the informational content even though the spatial context is lost.
findings.evidence: PDF reading order is spatial/geographic. HTML linearizes to: header info → legend → sample tables → notes.

findings.description: Two h1 elements present due to nested <main> tags. 'SURFACE WATER ASSESSMENT' is h1 and 'SOUTHSIDE PARK LANDFILL' is h2, which is acceptable structurally, but the HTML has two <main> elements nested inside each other.
findings.evidence: HTML contains <main> nested inside another <main>, and the document title 'SURFACE WATER ASSESSMENT' as h1 with 'SOUTHSIDE PARK LANDFILL' as h2. The PDF treats them both as title-level text.

findings.description: The 'Risk Assessment' row and CR/HI rows are placed as regular table rows rather than as a sub-header and data rows. While the data values are correct, the structure slightly misrepresents the original layout where 'Risk Assessment' was a label/header for the CR and HI values beneath it.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, 'Risk Assessment' is a label with CR and HI as sub-items. In the HTML tables, they appear as separate rows with empty second cells for 'Risk Assessment'.

findings.description: The notes section states standards are '15A NCAC 2B GROUNDWATER STANDARDS' in the HTML, which matches the PDF text exactly. However, this appears to be the PDF's own text - no issue here. The drawing path footer text is accurately reproduced.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML contain identical text for notes and references.

findings.description: The sample location identifiers (SW/SD-1 through SW/SD-5) appear both in the 'map-markers' section and in the legend section of the PDF. This is consistent with the PDF which shows them both on the map and in the legend, so this is an acceptable representation.
findings.evidence: SW/SD-1 through SW/SD-5 appear in the map-markers section and also referenced in legend as 'Surface Water and Sediment Sample Locations'.
